chapter: Chapter 2: ROLE OF THE GOVERNMENT IN HEALTH

questions.question: What is the definition of health according to the chapter?
questions.answer: Health means our ability to remain free of illness and injuries. However, health is not only about disease; it also includes mental well-being and living in a clean environment.

questions.question: What do people in a democracy expect from the government?
questions.answer: In a democracy, people expect the government to work for their welfare, specifically through the provision of education, health, employment, and infrastructure.

questions.question: Name two factors apart from disease that affect our health.
questions.answer: Two factors are clean drinking water and a pollution-free environment. Adequate food and a life without mental strain are also important.

questions.question: Does India have enough doctors and clinics?
questions.answer: Yes, India has a large number of doctors, clinics, and hospitals. It is among the largest producers of doctors in the world.

questions.question: What are Public Health Services?
questions.answer: Public health services are a chain of health centers and hospitals run by the government to provide treatment to all citizens.

questions.question: What are Private Health Facilities?
questions.answer: Private health facilities are health services that are not owned or run by the government. They are owned by individuals or companies.

questions.question: Who was Hakim Sheikh?
questions.answer: Hakim Sheikh was a member of the Paschim Banga Khet Mazdoor Samity (PBKMS) who fell off a running train and had a difficult time getting medical treatment.

questions.question: Why did many government hospitals refuse to admit Hakim Sheikh?
questions.answer: They refused him because they either did not have spare beds or did not have the specialized doctors and facilities necessary for his treatment.

questions.question: What did the Court say in the case of Hakim Sheikh?
questions.answer: The Court said that the state government must give Hakim Sheikh the money he had spent on his treatment because it is the duty of the government to provide necessary health services.

questions.question: Why is the public health system called 'public'?
questions.answer: It is called public because it is meant for all citizens and the government has established these hospitals to fulfill its commitment to provide healthcare to everyone.

questions.question: Where does the government get money for public health services?
questions.answer: The government uses the tax money paid by the public to fund and run these health services.

questions.question: What is one important function of public health services regarding diseases?
questions.answer: One important function is to take action to prevent the spread of diseases like TB, malaria, jaundice, cholera, and diarrhea.

questions.question: What is the full form of RMPs?
questions.answer: RMPs stands for Registered Medical Practitioners. They are commonly found in rural areas.

questions.question: Where are most private health facilities located?
questions.answer: Most private health facilities are concentrated in urban areas (cities) rather than rural areas.

questions.question: Is healthcare in India available to everyone equally?
questions.answer: No, healthcare is not available to everyone equally. Private services are increasing but are expensive, and public services are often insufficient.

questions.question: What is a major problem for poor people when they fall ill?
questions.answer: The major problem is the high cost of medicines and treatment, which often forces them to borrow money or sell their possessions.

questions.question: Define 'Medical Tourists'.
questions.answer: Medical tourists are people from other countries who come to India specifically for medical treatment at hospitals that offer world-class facilities at lower costs.

questions.question: What is the 'Costa Rican approach' to health?
questions.answer: Costa Rica decided not to have an army and used that money instead for health, education, and other basic needs of the people.

questions.question: What major step did the Kerala government take in 1996 for health?
questions.answer: The Kerala government gave 40% of the entire state budget to Panchayats so they could plan and provide for requirements like water, food, and education.

questions.question: What is the full form of OPD?
questions.answer: OPD stands for Out Patient Department. It is where people are first brought in and treated in a hospital without being admitted to a special ward.

questions.question: What are Communicable Diseases?
questions.answer: These are diseases that are spread from one person to another through water, food, or air (e.g., Hepatitis).

questions.question: Why is it important for the government to prevent the breeding of mosquitoes?
questions.answer: It is important because mosquitoes spread diseases like malaria and dengue, which affects the health of the entire community.

questions.question: State one reason why women often do not get prompt medical attention.
questions.answer: In many families, women's health concerns are considered less important than the health of men in the family.

questions.question: What is a 'Generic Medicine'?
questions.answer: Generic medicines are medications that have the same chemical ingredients as brand-name drugs but are sold at a much lower price.

questions.question: According to the Constitution, whose primary duty is it to ensure welfare and health?
questions.answer: It is the primary duty of the government to ensure the welfare of the people and provide health care facilities to all.
